key_insight: PA-SciML makes agentic SciML discovery verification-first by requiring each candidate surrogate to satisfy explicit physics checks, not just achieve low error.
engineering_relevance: For solid mechanics, this helps ensure surrogate models respect constraints like boundary conditions, stiffness scaling, superposition, and causality before they are trusted in analysis or design.
method: Fix the evaluator and physics requirements before search
Check each trained candidate on its predicted fields against reviewable, machine-checkable physics tests
Optionally probe input/load-history ranges for high-violation cases and test one modeling change at a time
evidence_signal: In the reported elasticity and elastodynamics examples, the physics-audited workflow selected surrogates that passed the stated checks, while an error-only baseline failed a stricter causality test despite similar mean error.
watch_item: Physics verification depends on the completeness and correctness of the prescribed checks, so missing requirements or weak test coverage could still allow unphysical surrogates to pass.
paper_title: Physics-Audited Agentic Discovery in Scientific Machine Learning
